content: Older adult health assessment includes: (1) Functional status—Activities of Daily Living (ADLs: bathing, dressing, eating, toileting, transferring), Instrumental ADLs (managing medications, finances, transportation), gait speed (<0.8 m/s indicates frailty); (2) Geriatric syndromes—frailty (Fried phenotype, Clinical Frailty Scale), fall risk (Timed Up and Go, STEADI algorithm), cognitive impairment (Mini-Cog, MoCA, MMSE), depression (Geriatric Depression Scale), delirium (CAM); (3) Multimorbidity assessment—disease count, weighted indices (Charlson Comorbidity Index), patient-reported burden; (4) Polypharmacy review—medication count (>5 indicates polypharmacy), Beers Criteria for potentially inappropriate medications, drug-drug interactions; (5) Social determinants—social isolation (UCLA Loneliness Scale), caregiver strain, food insecurity, financial toxicity; (6) Population indicators—life expectancy, healthy life expectancy, disability-free life expectancy, long-term care utilization. Limitations include underrepresentation of oldest-old in research, performance bias in those with disabilities, and need for proxy respondents with cognitive impairment.
